title: Peter Mata'utia

[[Pat Mata'utia]] (brother)
[[Sione Mata'utia]] (brother)
[[Masada Iosefa]] (cousin) }} }} '''Peter Mata'utia-Leifi''' ({{IPA|sm|mɑːtæutiæ}};{{Cite web|date=2014-07-24|title=Double debut for Mata'utias|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2014/07/24/double-debut-for-matautias/|website=Newcastle Knights}} born {{birth date|df=yes|1990|11|12}}), also known by the [[nickname]] of '''"Peanut"''',{{Cite web|date=2020-03-17|title=Richardson on Saints reunion win|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=6857|access-date=2020-06-09|website=Castleford Tigers}} is a [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce|fb}} or {{rlp|so}} for the [[Lakes United Seagulls]] in the [[Newcastle Rugby League]]{{cite web|website=Newcastle Weekly|date=7 Dec 2023|title=REAL NRL: Lakes United Seagulls swoop on Mata’utia|url=https://newcastleweekly.com.au/real-nrl-lakes-united-seagulls-swoop-on-matautia/}} in the [[Newcastle Rugby League]]. He has previously played for the [[Newcastle Knights]], in two spells, and the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]]. Mata'utia has also played for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[Betfred Championship|Championship]] and on loan from [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] at [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in the [[Super League]]. Mata'utia is signed to Warrington Wolves for the 2022 Superleague season. ==Background== Mata'utia was born in [[Bankstown|Bankstown, New South Wales]], Australia and is of [[Samoans|Samoan]] descent.{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/matautias-choose-family-over-country/tabid/10874/newsid/85876/default.aspx|title=Mata'utias choose family over country|date=May 2015|publisher=National Rugby League|access-date=2016-04-19}} He played his junior rugby league for the Bankstown Cougars. He then moved to [[Newcastle, New South Wales]], and played for [[Raymond Terrace]], before being signed by the [[Newcastle Knights]]. Mata'utia is the older brother of Knights teammates [[Chanel Mata'utia]], [[Pat Mata'utia]] and [[Sione Mata'utia]].[http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2014/07/23/sione_mata_utia_set_to_debut.html Sione's set to debut] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141030121857/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2014/07/23/sione_mata_utia_set_to_debut.html |date=30 October 2014 }}. ''newcastleknights.com.au''. 23 July 2014{{cite news|last=Dillon|first=Robert|date=19 March 2012|title=Bennett lays down law to drunken Knight Mata'utia|newspaper=The Newcastle Herald|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/114558/bennett-lays-down-law-to-drunken-knight-matautia/}} ==Playing career== [[File:PETER_MATA'UTIA.jpg|thumb|left|Mata'utia playing for the Knights in 2009.|150px]] ===Early career=== From 2008 to 2010, Mata'utia played for the Newcastle Knights' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team,{{cite web|url=https://nycdatabase.org/players/m/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150622220803/http://nycdatabase.org/players/m/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=22 June 2015|title=M|date=27 December 2012|publisher=Nyc Database|access-date=2016-04-19}} scoring over 300 points in 44 games.{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com//db/player/m/matautia_peter/index.php|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130205083855/http://www.rleague.com//db/player/m/matautia_peter/index.php|archive-date=5 February 2013|title=rleague.com – Mata'utia Peter|url-status=dead|access-date=1 June 2016}} In August 2008, he played for the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]].{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsportspulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=1-2130-0-0-0&sID=26416&articleID=5958315&news_task=DETAIL|title=2008 Australian Schoolboys Team Announced – Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|publisher=Fox Sports Pulse|date=2008-09-20|access-date=2016-04-19}}{{cite web|url=http://www.ourfootyteam.com/assrl_08Archives.php|title=A.S.S.R.L., Australian Secondary School Rugby League, Australian Schoolboys|publisher=Ourfootyteam.com|access-date=2016-04-19}} On 17 October 2009, he played for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] against the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] in the [[2009 Pacific Cup]].{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/2009-pacific-cup-squads/tabid/10874/newsid/57082/default.aspx|title=2009 Pacific Cup: Squads|date=10 October 2009|publisher=National Rugby League|access-date=2016-04-19}} ===2011=== In 2011, Mata'utia graduated to the Knights' [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW|New South Wales Cup]] team, [[Central Coast Centurions]]. In Round 11 of the [[2011 NRL season]], he made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Knights against the [[Sydney Roosters]],{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2011/round-11/sydney-vs-newcastle/summary.html|title=NRL 2011 – Round 11 – Rugby League Project|first=Shawn Dollin and Andrew|last=Ferguson|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=10 October 2017}} scoring a try on debut.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/sydney-roosters-host-newcastle-knights-in-round-11-nrl-clash-at-sydney-football-stadium/story-fn2mcuj6-1226060534071|title=Home | Live Scores & Latest News|publisher=Fox Sports|access-date=2016-04-19}} On 17 June, he re-signed with the Knights on a 2-year contract, turning down offers from three rival teams.{{cite news|last=Dillon|first=Robert|title=Mata'uti opts to stay in Hunter|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/471576/matauti-opts-to-stay-in-hunter/|newspaper=The Newcastle Herald|date=16 June 2011}} He finished his debut season having played in five games and scoring two tries.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtODE5LTQtLS0tLS0xNzE5Mi0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0=|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=2016-04-19}} ===2012=== [[File:petermata'utia2012.jpg|thumb|left|Mata'utia playing for the Knights in 2012]] In July, Mata'utia was admitted to hospital after suffering a crushed [[larynx]] in a New South Wales Cup game for the Knights. He received an emergency [[Tracheotomy|tracheostomy]] after he was unable to breathe during a scan on his neck. He was ruled out for the rest of the season,{{cite news|last=Toohey|first=Barry|title=Newcastle Knights utility Peter Mata'utia cheats death|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/newcastle-knights-utility-peter-matautia-cheats-death/story-e6frexq9-1226433382951|newspaper=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|date=24 July 2012}} after only playing one NRL game for the Knights that year.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTUzMC00LS0tLS0tMTcxOTItLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=2016-04-19}} ===2013=== On 17 July, Mata'utia played for the [[New South Wales Residents rugby league team|New South Wales Residents]] against the [[Queensland Residents rugby league team|Queensland Residents]].{{cite web|publisher=NSW Rugby League|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2013/07/07/vb-nsw-cup-rep-squad-named.html|title=VB NSW Cup Rep Squad named|access-date=2016-04-19|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304055919/http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2013/07/07/vb-nsw-cup-rep-squad-named.html|archive-date=4 March 2016}}{{cite news|last=Dillon|first=Robert|title=Mantellato has a point to prove|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/1633103/mantellato-has-a-point-to-prove/|newspaper=The Newcastle Herald|date=11 July 2013}} On 23 August, he signed a 2-year contract with the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] starting in 2014, after failing to break into the Knights' first-grade team in 2013.{{cite web|author=Dragons.com.au|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/dragons-add-depth-for-2014-10868/#uHoEJSyVVwKR62S3.97|title=Dragons add depth for 2014|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2013-08-23|access-date=2016-04-19}}{{cite web|url=http://www.dragons.com.au/news-display/dragons/82629 |title=Dragons Add Depth For 2014 |access-date=23 August 2013 |url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130826063352/http://www.dragons.com.au/news-display/dragons/82629|archive-date=26 August 2013}} On 22 September, he was named at centre in the 2013 New South Wales Cup Team of the Year.{{cite web|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/default.aspx?s%3Darticle-display%26id%3D84880%26title%3D2013-nswrlcomau-vb-nsw-cup-team-of-the-year---announcement |title=2013 NSWRL.com.au VB NSW Cup Team of the Year – Announcement |access-date=4 February 2016 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140111155627/http://www.nswrl.com.au/default.aspx?s=article-display&id=84880&title=2013-nswrlcomau-vb-nsw-cup-team-of-the-year---announcement |archive-date=11 January 2014}} ===2014=== On 3 May, Mata'utia returned to the Samoan side against [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] in the 2014 Pacific Rugby League International.{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/nrl-big-guns-for-samoa,-fiji-teams/tabid/10874/newsid/77980/default.aspx|title=NRL big guns for Samoa, Fiji teams|publisher=National Rugby League|access-date=2016-04-19|archive-date=27 April 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160427210134/http://www.nrl.com/nrl-big-guns-for-samoa,-fiji-teams/tabid/10874/newsid/77980/default.aspx|url-status=dead}} In Round 10 of the [[2014 NRL season]], he made his club debut for the Dragons against the [[Parramatta Eels]].{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2014/round-10/parramatta-vs-st-george-illawarra/summary.html|title=NRL 2014 – Round 10|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2014-05-17|access-date=2016-04-19}} On 8 September, he was named in the Samoan train-on squad for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]],[http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/samao-train-on-squad-announced-14837/ Samoa train on squad announced] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141014081012/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/samao-train-on-squad-announced-14837/ |date=14 October 2014 }}. ''Zero Tackle''. 8 September 2014 later making the final 24-man squad for Samoa,[https://www.nrl.com/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/82434/default.aspx Toa Samoa name Four Nations squad]. ''NRL.com''. 7 October 2014{{cite web|author=Zero Tackle|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad-15250/|title=Toa Samoa name Four Nations squad|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2014-10-07|access-date=2016-03-13|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160203031215/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad-15250/|archive-date=3 February 2016}} however he was unable to compete in the tournament due to passport issues.[https://www.dragons.com.au/news/2014/11/20/mata_utia_putting_in_the_hard_yards_early.html Mata'utia: Putting In The Hard Yards Early]. ''dragons.com.au''. 20 November 2014 ===2015=== [[File:Peter Mata'utia.jpg|thumb|right|Mata'utia playing for the Leigh Centurions in 2018]] On 3 May, Mata'utia again played for the New South Wales Residents against the Queensland Residents, this time alongside his brother Chanel.{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/85557/default.aspx|title=Representative Round team lists|publisher=National Rugby League|date=2015-04-28|access-date=2015-05-03}} On 24 June, he re-signed with the Dragons on a 2-year contract.{{cite web|author=Dragons.com.au|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/dragons-re-sign-peter-matautia-17946/#0l07z4cJzvRTGOw4.97|title=Dragons re-sign Peter Mata'utia|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2015-06-24|access-date=2016-04-19}} ===2016=== On 20 April, Mata'utia returned to the Newcastle Knights effective immediately on a contract until the end of 2017, after being released from his Dragons contract.{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/knights-sign-peter-matautia-21467/|title=Knights sign Peter Mata'utia|date=20 April 2016|publisher=Zero Tackle|access-date=2016-04-20}}{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/peter-matautia-leaves-dragons/tabid/10874/newsid/95039/default.aspx|title=Peter Mata'utia leaves Dragons|publisher=National Rugby League|date=2016-02-28|access-date=2016-04-20}} ===2017=== Mata'utia played in every match for the Knights in the 2017 season, only missing 6 games since returning to the Knights midway through the 2016 season. In October, he rejected a 1-year extension offer from the Knights and instead signed a 3-year contract with the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the English [[Championship (rugby league)|Kingstone Press Championship]].{{cite web|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/peter-matautia-signs-leigh-29735/|title=Peter Mata'utia signs with Leigh Centurions – Zero Tackle|date=11 October 2017|access-date=11 October 2017}} ===2018=== Mata'utia started the 2018 season with the [[Leigh Centurions]], however, was allowed to leave the club due to the club's inability to make the top 4 of the Championship and owner Derek Beaumont admitting that he would not be able to financially support the club beyond the current season.{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/34439/11448487/derek-beaumont-to-quit-leigh-centurions-after-failing-to-earn-super-league-spot|title = Derek Beaumont to quit Leigh Centurions after failing to earn Super League spot}} Mata'utia signed a loan deal with [[Castleford Tigers]] until the end of 2018, which would lead to a 3-year deal from 2019 until the end of 2021.{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/44966441|title=Peter Mata'utia: Castleford sign Samoan international full-back from Leigh|publisher=BBC Sport|date=26 July 2018|access-date=26 September 2018}} He made his debut in the Tigers' 52–24 victory over [[Widnes Vikings]].{{Cite news | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league | title=Rugby League – BBC Sport | publisher=BBC Sport }} ===2019=== Mata'utia was assigned the number 1 jersey.{{cite web |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-2019-squad-numbers-jake-trueman-gets-no-6-shirt/ |title=Castleford 2019 squad numbers: Jake Trueman gets No. 6 shirt |publisher=I Love Rugby League |date=7 January 2019 |access-date=19 October 2022}} In January 2019 he revealed tha this five-year-old son had suffered a cancer scare.{{cite web |url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/tigers-star-peter-matautia-ready-13831132 |title=Tigers star Peter Mata’utia ready to roar after dealing with family cancer scare |work=Mirror|date=8 January 2019 |author=Gareth Walker |access-date=19 October 2022}} ===2021=== On [[International rugby league in 2021#June|25 June 2021]] he played for the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]], and scored a try, in their 26–24 victory over [[England national rugby league team|England]], staged at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]], [[Warrington]], as part of England's [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] preparation. On 17 July, he played for Castleford in their [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] loss against St. Helens.{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57828390|title=Challenge Cup: St Helens beat Castleford 26-12 for first Wembley victory since 2008| publisher=BBC Sport }} ===2023=== He played 26 games for Warrington in the 2023 Super League season as Warrington finished sixth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in the clubs elimination playoff loss against St Helens.{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/23827325.highs-lows-warrington-wolves-2023-season/|title=Highs and lows from Warrington Wolves' 2023 season|website=warringtonguardian.co.uk}} On 7 October 2023, he announced his retirement from professional rugby league.{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=7 October 2023|accessdate=8 October 2023|title=Peter Mata’utia announces retirement: 'I look back with gratitude and appreciation for the 16 years I was blessed with'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/peter-matautia-announces-retirement-i-look-back-with-gratitude-and-appreciation-for-the-16-years-i-was-blessed-with}} ==References== {{reflist}}
